entry.title: Washington Post: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Griner to testify in Moscow; Ukraine hits Russian supply link in Kherson
  • 1,850-year-old Roman coin featuring zodiac sign found off Israeli coast
  • Stop the ‘steal’? Brazil’s Bolsonaro looks poised to start it.
  • David Trimble, peacemaker in Northern Ireland, dies at 77
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Russia again targets Black Sea region amid grain deal fears; E.U. plans to ration gas
  • Pentagon calls out China’s military threats as Taiwan tensions worsen
  • Kyaw Min Yu, Myanmar activist known as Ko Jimmy, executed at 53
  • Russia says it’ll withdraw from International Space Station after 2024
  • Boris Johnson idolized Churchill. U.K.’s next leader may look to Thatcher.
  • E.U. agrees to watered-down gas-rationing plan after early resistance
  • Myanmar junta defiantly defends executions as U.S. presses China to do more
  • After Russian port strike, Ukraine grain deal hangs in the balance
  • Droughts and more wildfires may be next for Britain after record heat wave
  • Brittney Griner prepares to testify this week in Russian trial
  • Amnesty: Taliban crackdown on rights is 'suffocating' women
  • Strong quake kills 2, injures dozens in northern Philippines
  • Argentines yearn for Evita, 70 years after her death
  • At UN, Iraq and Turkey escalate dispute over deadly attack
  • 15 killed, 50 injured in anti-UN protests in Congo's east
  • Pope in Canada prays for healing for 'terrible' colonization
  • Polish institute classifies cats as alien invasive species
  • Saudi crown prince: First EU visit since Khashoggi killing
  • Peru's struggling president marks his first year in office
  • Russia to drop out of International Space Station after 2024
  • ‘Black Panther’ sequel: A rebuke of misogyny and racism in 2 minutes
  • National Guard member sought out extremists, planned attack on police
  • Uvalde school board calls on Texas to raise minimum age for assault weapons
  • Third set of human remains recovered at shrinking Lake Mead, park says
  • GOP lawmaker opposed same-sex marriage, then went to gay son’s wedding
  • As Sandy Hook defamation trial begins, Alex Jones might be absent
  • Med students walk out mid-ceremony to protest speaker’s antiabortion views
  • Preacher and his wife robbed of $1 million in jewelry during sermon
  • A town didn’t have a veterans memorial. So this teen designed and built one.
  • Post Politics Now: Trump returns to Washington; Biden on cusp of some wins
  • Justice Dept. investigating Trump’s actions in Jan. 6 criminal probe
  • D.C. man is 2nd to receive longest sentence in Jan. 6 police assault
  • Biden poised for big wins in Congress
  • This company helps small businesses offer abortion travel coverage
  • Walmart cut its profit outlook. Here’s why that might worry rivals.
  • Inflation: How you can beat it — or even benefit from it
  • China targeted the Fed for more than a decade, Senate report finds
  • The changing shape of inflation
  • Thanks to technology, you can now own the rights to an iconic Biggie rap
  • Twitter schedules shareholder vote for embattled Elon Musk deal
  • Nature preserve is pleasant surprise for some Broadlands, Va., buyers
  • How to create a low-maintenance water feature on your property
  • Cooling housing market brings relief to buyers
  • Europe Is Faking Solidarity, and Putin Knows It
  • Boris Johnson Isn’t Going Away — and Tories Know It
  • DeFi And Degrowth: Or How Not to Fix Capitalism
  • With Draghi Gone, Putin May Make Moves on Italy
  • How Europe Became So Dependent on Putin for Its Gas
  • Why Blackouts Are Still Crippling South Africa
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution
  • How Italy’s Political Drama Forced an Election
  • Lucky for Whitmer, the moderate Michigan GOP is an endangered species
  • Why do people believe the ‘big lie’? Because Americans don’t trust the media.
  • Congress, this is your last chance to close the Medicaid coverage gap
  • Pence gets dissed again — and he ‘deserves it’
  • At Starbucks and beyond, American workers are standing up for themselves
  • If Democrats are losing the working class, it’s not because of the left
  • Democrats could pass a huge achievement — if they rally behind this measure
  • Without pensions, future retirees face financial trouble. Where’s Washington?
  • Mayor Bowser needs to take action on migrant buses coming to D.C.
  • Clinics where abortion is legal are at a breaking point. They need help.
  • If GOP senators don’t fear this vote, what could possibly scare them?
  • How to stop Trump’s sneak attack on the civil service
  • Born-again Pence
  • No one is above the law
  • The Catholic Church can do more to address crimes against Indigenous Peoples
  • There is no time to waste and no safe amount of fossil fuels
  • The EPA wasn’t stripped of power
  • Investigate the Secret Service
  • A Trump spokesman wins second place for chutzpah
  • The Commanders make the wrong choice for training
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 27 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-27 07:24AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • ‘If you cannot see it, you cannot be it’: Why representation matters to new Greens MP
  • Australia news LIVE: Inflation hits 6.1 per cent; Pauline Hanson snubs Indigenous acknowledgement, dismisses validity as Labor introduces climate bill to parliament
  • Magnitude-7 earthquake hits Philippines’ most populous island
  • Melbourne High’s former business manager admits stealing more than $350,000
  • ‘Pain that I can’t explain’: Youth leader allegedly stabbed by NRL player
  • The ‘ugly cry’ is trending. I’m glad because I’ve needed to do it too
  • Dockers must get used to Optus Stadium chopped-up turf for Demons clash: Longmuir
  • Western Force veteran Prior signs on for two more seasons
  • Dozens killed and injured in ‘social media-fuelled’ anti-UN protests
  • Rio Tinto halves dividend as falling iron ore price hits profit
  • China wants to ‘bully and dominate’ the Indo-Pacific: top US general
  • Chalmers faces his bleak Thursday but it’s no Black Tuesday
  • Great demand but less fortune for Morrison on Pentecostal speakers circuit
  • Petrol, construction costs push inflation up to a ‘confronting’ 6.1 per cent
  • Why can’t anyone get an answer in question time?
  • The latest illustrations from artist Matt Golding
  • The latest illustrations from artist Simon Letch
  • Best of cartoons, July 27, 2022
  • Our politics is suddenly boring, and that’s worth celebrating
  • Labor women’s caucus leader vows to push for equal, affordable access to abortion
  • How many city slickers used COVID for sea and tree change
  • Rates have to climb but recession risks are growing: IMF
  • The latest illustrations from artist Cathy Wilcox
  • The latest illustrations from artist Andrew Dyson
  • Best of cartoons, July 26, 2022
  • ‘Devastating impact’: PM rejects Greens call to halt fossil fuel exports
  • ‘Bodies in the streets, cars in trees’: Zoe Daniel on the disaster that put climate change on her radar
  • So much for mutual obligation: New job, first day, no show
  • Sydney Harbour Tunnel tolls to stay, deeper tunnel possible for new crossing
  • ‘Inevitable step forward’: Cranbrook’s high school to become fully co-ed
  • ‘We don’t know a lot about them’: Police identify sisters found dead in Sydney unit
  • Barilaro roadshow keeps crashing Perrottet’s trade mission
  • Second man charged over ‘meticulous assassination’ of Mahmoud ‘Brownie’ Ahmad
  • No pride for jersey boys, but you can bet on them
  • In the Herald: July 27, 1960
  • From the Archives, 1957: I talked to stay-in miners
  • Time to give the Commonwealth Games the boot
  • Boyfriend of murdered German backpacker to be extradited to NSW
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • US Justice Department probing Trump’s efforts to overturn election
  • Russia to drop out of International Space Station after 2024
  • ‘Oh my god’: Presenter faints, ending Tory leadership debate
  • Birmingham Games set to challenge British colonialism, celebrate diversity
  • China to raise concerns over AUKUS submarine deal at UN treaty meeting
  • Top bankers, business leaders say Brazil’s democracy in ‘grave danger’
  • ‘Risk is clear’: China seeks to infiltrate the US Federal Reserve, senior Senate Republican says
  • China warns of ‘serious consequences’ if Pelosi visits Taiwan
  • RMIT calls in lawyers as staff prepare to boycott university open days
  • Slow lifts, concrete bunker: Melbourne Aquarium left me feeling crabby
  • From the Archives, 1962: Puffing Billy makes triumphant comeback
  • Public sector pay stoush set to escalate after Perth cost of living soars
  • ‘Unacceptable’: NBN Co sent back to square one on pricing model
  • Instant gratification: The lure of a 10-minute mortgage approval
  • ‘Critical’: Westpac unveils new targets to cut carbon emissions
  • ‘Ansett old boys’ club’: Virgin pilot details Hrdlicka bullying claims
  • Pulling the strings: Putin is gaming Europe’s energy crisis
  • The Wrap: ASX undaunted by 20-year high inflation figures
  • Credit Suisse CEO set to exit as upheaval continues at scandal-plagued giant
  • World’s richest family loses $16 billion in one day in Walmart rout
  • Mark Zuckerberg’s biggest bet is not paying off
  • Inflation is not a big problem, so don’t hit it with a sledgehammer
  • Tabcorp’s head of wagering and media resigns
  • Power giants want new projects prioritised as coal is phased out
  • Westpac to offer 10-minute mortgage as it eyes refinancing surge
  • Pilot, Juniper healthcare brands to cut staff after investor pulls loan deal
  • A true chippie off the old Block: Scott Cam builds his own house
  • Morals and beliefs are on the line in uncompromising prison drama Time
  • Bob Marley to U2: Music trailblazer tells all in celebrity-strewn memoir
  • Dan Hong searches for the world’s greatest street foods
  • What it was like on Neighbours if you weren’t a star
  • The precarious balance of a woman’s life, and the speed it can unravel
  • By the end, I was long past caring about this film’s mirthless chuckles
  • Your ABC Exposed doco lands some punches but exposes little more than an agenda
  • Gorillaz stage a feel-good pop party with feel-bad undercurrents
  • Knives Out lookalike puts a French twist on the murder mystery
  • Is the future of Australian TV in jeopardy?
  • ‘I hope you took acid’ – Strokes deliver weird, robotic post-Splendour show
  • How Kate Miller-Heidke stuns the crowd into silence
  • You’d need a heart of stone not to be moved by Couples Therapy Australia
  • Tim Winton tackles an unsettling new project, 40 years after his acclaimed debut
  • Pioneering ‘champagne comedy’ stands the test of time – mostly
  • Instagram to Jenner: We’re not turning back on ‘TikTok’ changes
Breaking news from Sydney, Australia and the world. Features the latest business, sport, entertainment, travel, lifestyle, and technology news.

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 27 Jul, 2022 published at 2022-07-27 07:08AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Hong Kong poised to leap ahead, in ‘enviable’ spot as East-meets-West hub, John Lee says as he hails city’s talent and resilience
  • Raising Hong Kong’s plastic bag levy from proposed HK$1 to HK$2 will further reduce usage by 40 per cent, lawmaker says
  • Hong Kong’s lived-in home prices slump to lowest level since December 2020 as caution prevails ahead of rate hike
  • Coronavirus: arrivals under hotel quarantine in Hong Kong ‘can upload daily test results on website’ instead of filling out form in system trial
  • Hong Kong to launch HK$5 billion tech fund in August, finance chief reveals
  • Call for safety measures after member of popular Hong Kong boy band Mirror falls off stage
  • Hong Kong must raise interest rates but wild swing in property prices unlikely, finance chief says
  • To chart a new economic course for Hong Kong, build a new north-south rail line
  • Hong Kong leader John Lee, top judge and Olympic medallists among 890 named on city’s honours list
  • Hong Kong Book Fair 2022 closes with total of 850,000 visitors, a slight increase from last year’s event
  • Former CEO of China Everbright’s financial arm under investigation by Communist Party’s anti-corruption body
  • Hong Kong’s Exchange Fund to report ‘significant’ first-half loss as ‘perfect storm’ sends all assets into free fall, HKMA chief warns
  • Exclusive clubs took millions of Hong Kong dollars in wage subsidies designed to help cash-strapped firms during Covid fifth wave
  • Hong Kong court sentences 67-year-old man to life imprisonment for murdering wife after groceries dispute
  • Hong Kong and Shenzhen exchange operators join hands to promote Greater Bay Area as economic powerhouse, green finance hub
  • Hong Kong man who killed cancer-stricken wife pleads guilty to reduced charge of manslaughter
  • Hong Kong’s iconic The Peak Lookout restaurant has its rent slashed in half as pandemic wipes out international tourism
  • Hong Kong protests: magistrate committed ‘serious error’ by sending man guilty of assaulting officer to psychiatric centre, High Court judges find
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong health experts call for doubling of antiviral distribution rate for elderly as city logs 4,276 cases
  • Hong Kong police officer draws gun to arrest knife-wielding man, 77, suspected of robbing shop for pack of cigarettes
  • Should we worry about space debris from Chinese rockets hitting Earth?
  • Croatia opens China-built bridge that bypasses Bosnia to get to Dubrovnik – a boon for tourists, businesses and residents
  • Beijing says it seeks peaceful reunification with Taiwan, but warns island over separatist forces
  • China’s industrial profits rise for first time since March as coronavirus curbs ease, but concerns remain
  • How Nasa’s James Webb and China’s Xuntian space telescopes could cooperate in future
  • China orders Covid-19 community helpers to follow local leadership
  • Beijing to shake up housing market near elite schools with new education policy
  • New Covid-19 origins studies point to Wuhan market, not Chinese lab leak
  • Beyond China: US’ Xinjiang cotton ban has far-reaching implications, even for Asian alternatives
  • George Gao Fu, head of China’s CDC who helped lead coronavirus pandemic response, stepping down
  • China rejects criticism that its zero-Covid policies violate the rights of US diplomats
  • Taiwan visit by Nancy Pelosi risks sparking US-PLA conflict, army watchers warn
  • Widodo’s ‘balancing act’ meeting Xi in Beijing ahead of G20
  • China’s top chip maker SMIC may have achieved tech breakthrough, experts say
  • Chinese human rights lawyer Chang Weiping in closed-door trial as family barred from county
  • China-Indonesia trade: how important is it, and what are the main exports?
  • How artefacts are being protected at the Sanxingdui Ruins in China
  • China, Indonesia vow stronger ties as Xi Jinping and Joko Widodo hold ‘kind and friendly’ talks
  • China’s ‘worse-than-anticipated’ economic slowdown at core of IMF cut to 2022 global growth forecast
  • Chinese tech hub Shenzhen rolls out ‘targeted measures’ to reduce industrial disruptions, contain fresh Covid-19 outbreak
  • China may increase cultural imports as part of 28-point plan to boost its soft power overseas
  • Digital sentries were sent to curb Covid-19. Will they still be keeping watch when the pandemic is over?
  • Taiwanese President Tsai Ing-wen observes live-fire drill from warship
  • Singapore extends visit permit for Sri Lanka’s self-exiled former president Gotabaya Rajapaksa
  • Thailand cashes in on cannabis legalisation with surge in weed-infused products like milk tea and toothpaste
  • South Korea’s Yoon Suk-yeol takes aim at police as he battles record low approval ratings
  • Powerful 7-magnitude earthquake strikes the Philippines, killing at least 4
  • Indian actor Ranveer Singh’s nude photo shoot attracts police complaints: ‘This is vulgar. We can see his bum’
  • US officials call China’s actions in the South China Sea unsafe and increasing
  • More violence in Myanmar feared as junta’s executions send ‘ruthless’ message to Asean
  • Malaysia 1MDB scandal: Najib Razak hires new lawyer, seeks postponement of final appeal hearing
  • Russia doing better than expected despite sanctions over war in Ukraine, IMF says
  • Russia’s decision to drop out of International Space Station catches Nasa off guard. What now?
  • New pictures show Saudi plan for futuristic mirrored skyscraper megacity NEOM
  • Indian peacekeepers among 15 killed in anti-UN protests in Congo’s east
  • Ukraine war: An inside look at prison life for captured Russian troops
  • ‘Orwellian’ facial recognition surveillance cameras in UK’s Southern Co-operative stores challenged by rights group
  • Taliban crackdown on Afghan women escalates while world looks away, report finds
  • China tariff decision unlikely before call between Joe Biden and Xi Jinping
  • Donald Trump returns to Washington, hints at third presidential run
  • Meet Freya, the 600kg walrus sinking boats and capturing hearts in Europe
  • UK PM debate between Rishi Sunak and Liz Truss halted after host faints on air
  • Nato’s latest expansion plan could be beginning of the end as waning West targets ‘Russia threat’
  • Pet lovers upset after Polish institute classifies cats as ‘invasive alien species’
  • Russia to leave International Space Station after 2024 and build its own orbital outpost
  • European Union seals deal on reducing gas use as Russia squeezes supply
  • Germany’s Lufthansa cancels more than 1,000 flights amid ground staff strike for higher pay
  • Miniso loses US$265 million in stock market rout as short-seller Blue Orca attacks business model, insider ties
  • A developing-country debt crisis is looming, but it can be headed off
  • Binance’s founder CZ sues Modern Media for libel as the richest man in the cryptocurrency world steps up action to defend his reputation
  • Alibaba, HSBC pressure Hong Kong stocks before Fed decision while Country Garden sinks on stock placement
  • Thailand’s plan to allow foreigners own land may worsen oversupply of flats in Bangkok
  • China could have most to lose as crises end globalisation era
  • China’s searing heat wave strains power grid as demand for air conditioning soars past last year’s record
  • Kaisa takes baby steps towards repaying US$18 billion of borrowings, aims to publish overdue 2021 accounts by October
  • China’s top basketball league slaps streaming video giant Bilibili with copyright lawsuit, US$60.12 million in compensation
  • Shopify will cut 10 per cent of its staff after Covid-19 expansion bet fails to pay off
  • An island retreat with old Hong Kong vibes, rattan furniture and a rooftop for entertaining guests
  • ‘A sane person would have stopped’: Covid-19 news tweeter in Hong Kong, Tripperhead, on feeling burned out but compelled to carry on
  • Bruce Lee, Elvis and Jackie Chan at Madame Tussauds in Hong Kong – when the waxworks museum opened in 2000, and the figure who snubbed it
  • From Hello Kitty to Rolex watches, custom cakes by Hong Kong baker are so realistic you’ll do a double take
  • NewJeans vs Le Sserafim, NCT vs NCT: how K-pop labels manufacture in-house rivalry between groups to fatten their profits
  • DC League of Super-Pets movie review: Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart team up to save Superman in plot-heavy animated adventure
  • Do you have osteoarthritis in your knees and fear walking will cause pain? It actually strengthens the joints and lowers the risk of pain, study finds
  • She brought The Rolling Stones to Hong Kong: tributes flow for Colleen Ironside, music promoter
  • Is pregnancy glow just a myth? Why women can look better when expecting and skincare that can help if you’re not so lucky – expert advice
  • Meet rapper Eminem’s daughter, Hailie Jade Mathers – singing on her dad’s music earned her the Guinness World Record for youngest R&B chart entrant and now she hosts her own podcast
  • Meet Ann-Margret Olsson, Elvis Presley’s not-so-secret girlfriend: the Swedish Hollywood star played The King’s wife in Viva Las Vegas – but real-life fiancée Priscilla rightly had doubts
  • Who is Nina Agdal, Logan Paul’s rumoured girlfriend and Leonardo DiCaprio’s ex? The Danish bombshell modelled for Victoria’s Secret and once starred in a TV series with Kendall Jenner
  • 9 luxury hotels owned by celebrities: Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi run hospitality groups, as does Robert De Niro with Nobu – and Leonardo DiCaprio’s private island resort is also in the works
  • Who is Nicole Shanahan, Elon Musk’s alleged hook-up and Sergey Brin’s wife? The lawyer and fellow tech whizz is divorcing Google’s co-founder, but was accused of having an affair – which Musk denied
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: ChannelNewsAsia: 27 Jul, 2022
entry.content:

entry.content:

entry.content:

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
